# Quiz: Context-Free Grammars

**Q1. Which language can be generated by a CFG but NOT by any regular expression?**
- [ ] {w | w contains the substring "ab"}
- [x] {aⁿbⁿ | n ≥ 0}
- [ ] {w | w ends in 0}
- [ ] All strings over {a, b}

**Q2. In Chomsky Normal Form (CNF), every production rule has the form:**
- [ ] A → w where w ∈ Σ* (arbitrary terminal string)
- [x] A → BC (two variables) or A → a (one terminal), plus S → ε only if ε ∈ L(G)
- [ ] A → aB or A → a (right-linear rules only)
- [ ] A → B | a (variable or single terminal)

**Q3. A CFG is ambiguous if:**
- [ ] Its language contains the empty string
- [ ] It has more production rules than variables
- [x] Some string in its language has two distinct leftmost derivations from the start variable
- [ ] The start variable appears on the right-hand side of a rule

**Q4. In the CNF conversion, which step eliminates "unit rules" (rules of the form A → B)?**
- [x] Replace A → B combined with B → w₁ | w₂ | … by adding A → w₁ | w₂ | … directly, then remove A → B
- [ ] Replace A → B with A → Bε
- [ ] Rename variable B to A everywhere
- [ ] Unit rules are already in CNF and require no conversion

**Q5. Why is the CYK algorithm guaranteed to terminate in O(n³·|G|) time?**
- [ ] Because it uses a stack to track derivations
- [x] Because it fills an n×n triangular table, each entry taking O(n·|G|) work to compute via CNF rules A → BC
- [ ] Because CNF has at most n rules
- [ ] Because it processes the input left-to-right with a single pass

**Q6. Context-free languages are closed under which of the following?**
- [ ] Intersection of two CFLs
- [ ] Complement of a CFL
- [x] Union of two CFLs (by adding a new start rule S → S₁ | S₂)
- [ ] None of the above

**Q7. The grammar S → aSb | SS | ε generates:**
- [ ] All strings over {a, b}
- [ ] Only strings of the form aⁿbⁿ
- [x] All strings over {a, b} where every prefix has at least as many a's as b's (Dyck language / balanced strings)
- [ ] Only the empty string ε
